schema.description: The response from preparing a payroll for update. Contains refreshed employee compensations, updated payroll dates, and version information needed for subsequent payroll updates.

payroll_deadline.description: A timestamp that is the deadline for the payroll to be run in order for employees to be paid on time.  If payroll has not been run by the deadline, a prepare request will update both the check date and deadline to reflect the soonest employees can be paid and the deadline by which the payroll must be run in order for said check date to be met.
payroll_deadline.title: Payroll-Deadline-Type

check_date.description: The date on which employees will be paid for the payroll.
check_date.title: Payroll-Check-Date-Type

processed.description: Whether or not the payroll has been successfully processed. Note that processed payrolls cannot be updated. Additionally, a payroll is not guaranteed to be processed just because the payroll deadline has passed. Late payrolls are not uncommon. Conversely, users may choose to run payroll before the payroll deadline.
processed.title: Payroll-Processed-Type

processed_date.description: The date at which the payroll was processed. Null if the payroll isn't processed yet.
processed_date.title: Payroll-Processed-Date-Type

calculated_at.description: A timestamp of the last valid payroll calculation. Null if there isn't a valid calculation.
calculated_at.title: Payroll-Calculated-At-Type

company_uuid.description: The UUID of the company for the payroll.
company_uuid.title: Payroll-Company-Uuid-Type

off_cycle.description: Indicates whether the payroll is an off-cycle payroll
off_cycle.title: Payroll-Off-Cycle-Type

off_cycle_reason.description: The off-cycle reason. Only included for off-cycle payrolls.
off_cycle_reason.title: Off-Cycle-Reason-Type

auto_pilot.description: Indicates whether the payroll is an auto pilot payroll
auto_pilot.title: Auto-Pilot-Type

external.description: Indicates whether the payroll is an external payroll
external.title: Payroll-External-Type

final_termination_payroll.description: Indicates whether the payroll is the final payroll for a terminated employee. Only included for off-cycle payrolls.
final_termination_payroll.title: Payroll-Final-Termination-Payroll-Type

withholding_pay_period.description: The payment schedule tax rate the payroll is based on. Only included for off-cycle payrolls.
withholding_pay_period.title: Payroll-Withholding-Pay-Period-Type

skip_regular_deductions.description: Block regular deductions and contributions for this payroll.  Only included for off-cycle payrolls.
skip_regular_deductions.title: Payroll-Skip-Regular-Deductions-Type

fixed_withholding_rate.description: Enable taxes to be withheld at the IRS's required rate of 22% for federal income taxes. State income taxes will be taxed at the state's supplemental tax rate. Otherwise, we'll sum the entirety of the employee's wages and withhold taxes on the entire amount at the rate for regular wages. Only included for off-cycle payrolls.
fixed_withholding_rate.title: Payroll-Fixed-Withholding-Rate-Type

start_date.description: The start date, inclusive, of the pay period.

end_date.description: The start date, inclusive, of the pay period.

pay_schedule_uuid.description: The UUID of the pay schedule for the payroll.

payroll_status_meta.description: Information about the payroll's status and expected dates

cancellable.description: true if the payroll may be cancelled.

expected_check_date.description: The date an employee will be paid if the payroll is submitted now.

initial_check_date.description: The normal check date for the associated pay period.

expected_debit_time.description: The time the employer's account will be debited if the payroll is submitted now.

initial_debit_cutoff_time.description: Payroll must be submitted at or before this time to avoid late payroll.

payroll_status_meta.title: Payroll-Payroll-Status-Meta-Type

excluded.description: This employee will be excluded (skipped) from payroll calculation and will not be paid for the payroll. Cancelling a payroll would reset all employees' excluded back to false.

first_name.description: The first name of the employee. Requires `employees:read` scope.

preferred_first_name.description: The preferred first name of the employee. Requires `employees:read` scope.

last_name.description: The last name of the employee. Requires `employees:read` scope.

gross_pay.description: The employee's gross pay, equal to regular wages + cash tips + payroll tips + any other additional earnings, excluding imputed income. This value is only available for processed payrolls.

net_pay.description: The employee's net pay, equal to gross_pay - employee taxes - employee deductions or garnishments - cash tips. This value is only available for processed payrolls.

check_amount.description: The employee's check amount, equal to net_pay + reimbursements. This value is only available for processed payrolls.

payment_method.description: The employee's compensation payment method. Is *only* `Historical` when retrieving external payrolls initially run outside of Gusto, then put into Gusto.

memo.description: Custom text that will be printed as a personal note to the employee on a paystub.

fixed_compensations.description: An array of fixed compensations for the employee. Fixed compensations include tips, bonuses, and one time reimbursements. If this payroll has been processed, only fixed compensations with a value greater than 0.00 are returned. For an unprocessed payroll, all active fixed compensations are returned.

name.description: The name of the compensation. This also serves as the unique, immutable identifier for this compensation.

amount.description: The amount of the compensation for the pay period.

job_uuid.description: The UUID of the job for the compensation.

hourly_compensations.description: An array of hourly compensations for the employee. Hourly compensations include regular, overtime, and double overtime hours. If this payroll has been processed, only hourly compensations with a value greater than 0.00 are returned. For an unprocessed payroll, all active hourly compensations are returned.

name.description: The name of the compensation. This also serves as the unique, immutable identifier for this compensation.

hours.description: The number of hours to be compensated for this pay period.

amount.description: The amount of the compensation. This field is only available after the payroll is calculated and cannot be used for updating hourly compensations.

job_uuid.description: The UUID of the job for the compensation.

compensation_multiplier.description: The amount multiplied by the base rate to calculate total compensation per hour worked.

flsa_status.description: The FLSA Status of the employee's primary job compensation

paid_time_off.description: An array of all paid time off the employee is eligible for this pay period.

name.description: The name of the PTO. This also serves as the unique, immutable identifier for the PTO.

hours.description: The hours of this PTO taken during the pay period.

final_payout_unused_hours_input.description: The outstanding hours paid upon termination. This field is only applicable for termination payrolls.

reimbursements.description: An array of reimbursements for the employee.

amount.description: The dollar amount of the reimbursement for the pay period.

description.description: The description of the reimbursement. Null for unnamed reimbursements.

uuid.description: The UUID of the reimbursement. Null for unnamed reimbursements. This field is only available for unprocessed payrolls.

recurring.description: Whether the reimbursement is recurring. This field is only available for unprocessed payrolls.

version.description: The current version of the object. See the [versioning guide](https://docs.gusto.com/embedded-payroll/docs/idempotency) for information on how to use this field.

The current version of this employee compensation. This field is only available for prepared payrolls. See the [versioning guide](https://docs.gusto.com/embedded-payroll/docs/idempotency) for information on how to use this field.

deductions.description: An array of deductions for the employee. This field is included by default for regular payrolls in version `v2025-06-15` and later.

amount.description: The amount of the deduction for the pay period.

amount_type.description: The amount type of the deduction for the pay period. Only present for calculated or processed payrolls.

uuid.description: The UUID of the deduction. Only present for calculated or processed payrolls.

payment_speed_changed.description: Only applicable when a payroll is moved to four day processing instead of fast ach.

original_check_date.description: Original check date when fast ach applies.

original_debit_date.description: Original debit date when fast ach applies.

reason.description: The reason why the payroll is moved to four day.

payment_speed_changed.title: Payroll-Payment-Speed-Changed-Type

created_at.description: Datetime for when the resource was created.
created_at.title: Created-At-Type

name.description: The name of an available type of fixed compensation.

status.description: The status of the payroll processing request

errors.description: Errors that occurred during async payroll processing

error_key.description: Specifies where the error occurs. Typically this key identifies the attribute/parameter related to the error.

category.description: Specifies the type of error. The category provides error groupings and can be used to build custom error handling in your integration. If category is `nested_errors`, the object will contain a nested `errors` property with entity errors.

message.description: Provides details about the error - generally this message can be surfaced to an end user.

metadata.description: Contains relevant data to identify the resource in question when applicable. For example, to identify an entity `entity_type` and `entity_uuid` will be provided.

entity_type.description: Name of the entity that the error corresponds to.
Name of the entity that the error corresponds to.

errors.description: Will only exist if category is `nested_errors`. It is possible to have multiple levels of nested errors.

partner_owned_disbursement.description: Will money movement for the payroll be performed by the partner rather than by Gusto?
partner_owned_disbursement.title: Payroll-Partner-Owned-Disbursement-Type

schema.title: Payroll-Prepared

schema.description:
Not Found 
  
The requested resource does not exist. Make sure the provided ID/UUID is valid.

error_key.description: Specifies where the error occurs. Typically this key identifies the attribute/parameter related to the error.

category.description: Specifies the type of error. The category provides error groupings and can be used to build custom error handling in your integration.

message.description: Provides details about the error - generally this message can be surfaced to an end user.

errors.message: The requested resource was not found.

schema.title: Not-Found-Error-Object

422.description:
Unprocessable Entity

This may happen when the body of your request contains errors such as `invalid_attribute_value`, or the request fails due to an `invalid_operation`. See the [Errors Categories](https://docs.gusto.com/embedded-payroll/docs/error-categories) guide for more details.

schema.description:
Unprocessable Entity
  
This may happen when the body of your request contains errors such as `invalid_attribute_value`, or the request fails due to an `invalid_operation`. See the [Errors Categories](https://docs.gusto.com/embedded-payroll/docs/error-categories) guide for more details.

error_key.description: Specifies where the error occurs. Typically this key identifies the attribute/parameter related to the error.

category.description: Specifies the type of error. The category provides error groupings and can be used to build custom error handling in your integration. If category is `nested_errors`, the object will contain a nested `errors` property with entity errors.

message.description: Provides details about the error - generally this message can be surfaced to an end user.

metadata.description: Contains relevant data to identify the resource in question when applicable. For example, to identify an entity `entity_type` and `entity_uuid` will be provided.

entity_type.description: Name of the entity that the error corresponds to.
Name of the entity that the error corresponds to.

errors.description: Will only exist if category is `nested_errors`. It is possible to have multiple levels of nested errors.
